Registry / Terms / conventional true value (of a quantity)

conventional true value (of a quantity)

VIMOutdated — VIM 1993 G 18 #00571 · 10 instances

Authoritative definition

VIM 1993 · concept #1.20view ↗

value attributed to a particular quantity and accepted, sometimes by convention, as having an uncertainty appropriate for a given purpose

Outdated: Cites VIM 1993. Latest: VIM 2012.
  • VIM 1993 · #1.20
    value attributed to a particular quantity and accepted, sometimes by convention, as having an uncertainty appropriate for a given purpose
  • VIM 2007 · #2.12
    *quantity value* attributed by agreement to a *quantity* for a given purpose

Suggested actions

  1. HIGH ✗ This term is NOT in VIM 2012. It may have been removed, renamed, or superseded by a different concept.
  2. MEDIUM 10 distinct definitions — harmonise.
  3. LOW 7 partially diverge.

#02069

value attributed to a particular quantity and accepted, sometimes (VIM:2007, 2.12) by convention, as having an uncertainty appropriate for a given purpose
ok
